compiled_state.title: Contract freeze (a): canonical exit-code contract doc + conformance test
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
Sub-effort (a) of story fatty-cipher-range. Author the single canonical exit-code contract for all public dispatcher subcommands and pin it with a conformance test. Codes in use today: 0 (success), 1 (validation/input error), 2 (usage/parse error in some commands, e.g. clarity-check missing-ticket), 10 (optimistic-concurrency rejection from ticket_txn.py).

## Scope decision (recorded)
All 41 public dispatcher arms — the exact inventory is recorded in the implementation-notes comment on bug dotty-ditty-ripen (e14e-70b3): archive bridge-fsck bridge-status check-ac claim clarity-check comment compact compact-all create delete deps edit exists format fsck fsck-recover get-file-impact get-verify-commands init link list list-descendants list-epics next-batch purge-bridge quality-check ready reopen resolve revert scratch search set-file-impact set-verify-commands show summary tag transition unlink untag validate. Meta arm (help) excluded.

## What
- docs/exit-codes.md: per-code meaning + per-command table (source each row from the script headers/ticket_txn.py).
- tests/interfaces/test_exit_codes.py: conformance spot-checks for the load-bearing paths (transition concurrency -> 10, missing ticket -> 1, clarity-check missing -> 2, usage error -> documented code).

## Suggested RED test
The conformance test itself, written against the doc, RED wherever actual codes deviate from documented (each deviation is then a decision: fix code or fix doc — record which).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] docs/exit-codes.md covers all 41 arms with sourced codes
- [ ] Conformance test pins 0/1/2/10 on the load-bearing paths
- [ ] Deviations found during writing are each resolved with a recorded decision
